message:
feat(fp-frame): add reindex_with_method and groupby sort parameter

Add two pandas-compatible features to the fp-frame crate:

reindex_with_method() for Series and DataFrame:
  Combines reindex() with forward-fill or back-fill to handle NaN
  values introduced when new labels don't exist in the original index.
  Supports method="ffill"/"pad" and method="bfill"/"backfill",
  matching the pandas df.reindex(labels, method=...) API. Invalid
  method strings return a CompatibilityRejected error.

groupby sort parameter:
  Add groupby_full(by, as_index, sort) method that exposes the pandas
  sort parameter. When sort=True (the default, matching pandas),
  group keys are sorted lexicographically before aggregation. When
  sort=False, groups appear in first-seen order, which is useful for
  preserving insertion order and avoiding the sort overhead.

  The existing groupby() and groupby_with_as_index() methods now
  delegate to groupby_full() with sort=True, maintaining backward
  compatibility. The DataFrameGroupBy struct gains a sort field, and
  compute_groups() conditionally sorts group_order based on this flag.

  The existing groupby boolean aggregation test is updated to expect
  sorted key order ("False" < "True") which is now the correct
  default behavior.

Includes 5 new tests:
  - series_reindex_with_method_ffill: validates forward fill on gaps
  - series_reindex_with_method_bfill: validates backward fill on gaps
  - series_reindex_with_method_invalid_rejected: error on bad method
  - dataframe_reindex_with_method_ffill: validates DataFrame variant
  - dataframe_groupby_sort_false_preserves_first_seen_order: validates
    both sort=True and sort=False group ordering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
